title: faion-network methodology meta.json
description: Runtime metadata for a single methodology under skills/faion/knowledge/<domain>/<slug>/. Authoritative source for tier gating, search facets, and tier-manifest generation. Spec: .aidocs/conventions/meta-json-spec.md

slug.description: Methodology identifier; MUST equal the containing folder name.

tier.description: Pricing tier that gates access. Authoritative — replaces path-prefix inference.

domain.description: Canonical domain (one of the 20 listed in skills/faion/knowledge/domains.xml). MUST equal the first path segment under knowledge/.

group.description: Sub-cluster within the domain (matches L2 INDEX.xml grouping).

version.description: Semver of the methodology body + meta together. Bumped on every content edit per the evolution policy.

status.description: Lifecycle. Manifest builder excludes draft + deprecated from non-internal tier maps.

last_reviewed.description: ISO-8601 calendar date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the most recent review. Stale-after-180-days check runs in CI.

maintainers.description: Handles responsible for the methodology. Use ["faion-network"] for unattributed corpus content.

summary.description: One-sentence purpose statement. Used by L2 INDEX.xml + search snippets. Validator warns above 300 chars.

content_id.description: 16-character lowercase hex digest of the canonicalised content/*.xml payload. Used for cache busting + drift detection. Algorithm in meta-json-spec.md §6.

complexity.description: Cognitive depth tier. Used by retriever pre-filter + Task Routing in AGENTS.md.

produces.description: One-word artifact noun the methodology produces. Convention values: config, spec, report, code, decision, plan, test, doc. Used by retriever facet filter.

est_tokens.description: Estimated total tokens to fully load the methodology (AGENTS.md + all content/*.xml). Used by retriever budget planning.

tags.description: Free-form discovery tags (lowercase, kebab-case recommended). Used by faion search.

examples.summary: Spec for SKILL.md (frontmatter + body), tool-permission scope, global vs project-specific distinction, name conventions, auto-trigger keyword design.
